mn59:1.10: two *vedanās* were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*—
mn59:1.12: This neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha* *vedanā*, bhante, was spoken of by the *Bhagavā* as *sukha* in the sense that it is peaceful and sublime.”
mn59:1.2: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn59:1.3: Then Pañcakaṅga the carpenter approached Venerable Udāyī; having approached, after paying homage to Venerable Udāyī, he sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Pañcakaṅga the carpenter said this to Venerable Udāyī:
mn59:1.4: “How many *vedanās*, bhante Udāyī, were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*?”
mn59:1.5: “Three *vedanās*, carpenter, were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*.
mn59:1.7: these three *vedanās*, carpenter, were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*.”
mn59:1.8: When this was said, Pañcakaṅga the carpenter said this to Venerable Udāyī:
mn59:1.9: “No, bhante Udāyī, it is not three *vedanās* that were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*;
mn59:10.3: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:10.4: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, with the giving up of *sukha* … enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
mn59:10.5: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:11.3: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:11.4: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of *saññās* of *rūpa* [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the vanishing of *saññās* of resistance [*paṭighasaññānaṁ*], through non-attention to *saññās* of diversity [*nānattasaññānaṁ*], thinking, ‘space is boundless,’ enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn59:11.5: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:12.3: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:12.4: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, entirely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of boundless space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘consciousness [*viññāṇa*] is boundless,’ enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇa*] [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn59:12.5: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:13.3: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:13.4: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, entirely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of boundless consciousness [*viññāṇa*] [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘there is nothing,’ enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn59:13.5: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:14.3: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:14.4: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, entirely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn59:14.5: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:15.1: Whoever, Ānanda, might say: ‘This is the highest *sukha* and gladness that beings experience’—that I do not allow.
mn59:15.3: Because, Ānanda, there is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:15.4: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:15.5: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, entirely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*], enters and dwells in the cessation of *saññā* and *vedanā* [*saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ*].
mn59:15.6: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:16.1: “Now, Ānanda, it is possible that wanderers of other sects might say this:
mn59:16.2: ‘The ascetic Gotama speaks of the cessation of *saññā* and *vedanā* [*saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ*], and yet he describes that as *sukha*.
mn59:16.3: What is this? How is this?’
mn59:16.4: Wanderers of other sects who speak thus, Ānanda, should be told this:
mn59:16.5: ‘Friends, the *Bhagavā* does not describe something as *sukha* only with reference to *sukha* *vedanā*;
mn59:16.6: rather, friends, wherever *sukha* is found, in whatever case it is found, the Tathāgata describes just that as *sukha*.’”
mn59:16.8: Satisfied, Venerable Ānanda delighted in the *Bhagavā’s* words.
mn59:16.9: The Many Kinds of *vedanā*, the ninth, is finished.
mn59:2.1: A second time, Venerable Udāyī said this to Pañcakaṅga the carpenter:
mn59:2.10: This neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha* *vedanā*, bhante, was spoken of by the *Bhagavā* as *sukha* in the sense that it is peaceful and sublime.”
mn59:2.2: “No, householder, it is not two *vedanās* that were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*;
mn59:2.3: three *vedanās* were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*.
mn59:2.5: these three *vedanās*, carpenter, were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*.”
mn59:2.6: A second time, Pañcakaṅga the carpenter said this to Venerable Udāyī:
mn59:2.7: “No, bhante Udāyī, it is not three *vedanās* that were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*;
mn59:2.8: two *vedanās* were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*—
mn59:3.1: A third time, Venerable Udāyī said this to Pañcakaṅga the carpenter:
mn59:3.10: This neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha* *vedanā*, bhante, was spoken of by the *Bhagavā* as *sukha* in the sense that it is peaceful and sublime.”
mn59:3.11: Venerable Udāyī was unable to convince Pañcakaṅga the carpenter, and Pañcakaṅga the carpenter was unable to convince Venerable Udāyī.
mn59:3.2: “No, carpenter, it is not two *vedanās* that were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*;
mn59:3.3: three *vedanās* were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*.
mn59:3.5: these three *vedanās*, carpenter, were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*.”
mn59:3.6: A third time, Pañcakaṅga the carpenter said this to Venerable Udāyī:
mn59:3.7: “No, bhante Udāyī, it is not three *vedanās* that were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*;
mn59:3.8: two *vedanās* were spoken of by the *Bhagavā*—
mn59:4.1: Venerable Ānanda heard this discussion between Venerable Udāyī and Pañcakaṅga the carpenter.
mn59:4.2: Then Venerable Ānanda approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, after paying homage to the *Bhagavā*, he sat down to one side.
mn59:4.3: Sitting to one side, Venerable Ānanda reported to the *Bhagavā* the entire conversation that had taken place between Venerable Udāyī and Pañcakaṅga the carpenter.
mn59:4.4: When this was said, the *Bhagavā* said this to Venerable Ānanda:
mn59:5.1: “There is indeed, Ānanda, a mode of expression that Pañcakaṅga the carpenter did not approve in Udāyī, and there is indeed a mode of expression that Udāyī did not approve in Pañcakaṅga the carpenter.
mn59:5.2: By way of a mode of expression, Ānanda, two *vedanās* were spoken of by me; by way of a mode of expression, three *vedanās* were spoken of by me; by way of a mode of expression, five *vedanās* were spoken of by me; by way of a mode of expression, six *vedanās* were spoken of by me; by way of a mode of expression, eighteen *vedanās* were spoken of by me; by way of a mode of expression, thirty-six *vedanās* were spoken of by me; by way of a mode of expression, one hundred and eight *vedanās* were spoken of by me.
mn59:5.3: Thus, Ānanda, the *dhamma* has been taught by me through modes of expression.
mn59:5.4: When the *dhamma* has thus been taught by me through modes of expression, those who will not allow, accept, or approve what is well said and well spoken by one another may be expected to live quarreling, arguing, and disputing, wounding one another with verbal daggers.
mn59:5.5: Thus, Ānanda, the *dhamma* has been taught by me through modes of expression.
mn59:5.6: When the *dhamma* has thus been taught by me through modes of expression, those who will allow, accept, and approve what is well said and well spoken by one another may be expected to live in harmony, delighting together, without dispute, become like milk and water, looking at one another with kindly eyes.
mn59:6.1: These, Ānanda, are the five cords of sensual pleasure.
mn59:6.3: Forms cognizable by the eye, wished for, desired, agreeable, likable, connected with sensuality, enticing;
mn59:6.7: tangibles cognizable by the body, wished for, desired, agreeable, likable, connected with sensuality, enticing—
mn59:6.8: these, Ānanda, are the five cords of sensual pleasure.
mn59:6.9: Whatever *sukha* and gladness arises dependent on these five cords of sensual pleasure is called sensual *sukha*.
mn59:7.1: Whoever, Ānanda, might say: ‘This is the highest *sukha* and gladness that beings experience’—that I do not allow.
mn59:7.3: Because, Ānanda, there is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:7.4: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:7.5: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome *dhammas* … enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*.
mn59:7.6: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:8.1: Whoever, Ānanda, might say: ‘This is the highest *sukha* and gladness that beings experience’—that I do not allow.
mn59:8.3: Because, Ānanda, there is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:8.4: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:8.5: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, with the settling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*] … enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*.
mn59:8.6: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
mn59:9.3: And what, Ānanda, is the other *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*?
mn59:9.4: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu*, with the fading away of *pīti* … enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*.
mn59:9.5: This, Ānanda, is another *sukha* more excellent and more sublime than this *sukha*.
